input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Per-region competence not characterized for a region the COU depends on, despite a global in-envelope status.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40 applicability; NASA-STD-7009B; proto-taxonomy D-PD-02

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts blade metal temperature for a life-assessment decision
case_context.decision_consequence: component life margin set on the prediction

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The tip-gap region drives the heat-transfer QoI but is absent from the validation set, so local applicability is not established even though the global operating point is in-envelope.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the surrogate under-predicts tip temperature (a correctness verdict)
treats the global in-envelope status as competence for the tip-gap region

hardness.obvious_signal: global operating point sits well inside the validated envelope
hardness.conflicting_signal: the blade tip-gap region drives the heat-transfer QoI and was excluded from the validation set; local secondary-flow competence is uncharacterized

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 applicability + turbomachinery V&V practice: heat-transfer credibility is region-specific; global envelope status does not transfer to an unvalidated driver region.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
